experience: Attend the pre-dawn butter and cream auction at the Marché au Beurre in Isigny-sur-Mer, where Norman dairy cooperatives have traded AOC-designated butter and crème fraîche since the 19th century. Local producers and wholesalers haggle over lots before most tourists are awake.
when: Early Saturday mornings, spring through autumn
why_distinctive: The Isigny AOC designation is among the most protected in French dairy; the physical auction format where buyers smell and taste samples before bidding is a vanishing ritual almost entirely unvisited by outsiders.
source: Documented in Naomi Duguid and Jeffrey Alford's 'Seductions of Rice' travel essays and referenced in Patricia Wells's 'The Food Lover's Guide to France' (1987), which describes the cooperative trading culture of Isigny specifically.
